OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

  "opinion": "As a 90-year-old retired man from Kansas City who's been curious about the world but not deeply involved in modern technology, I've watched the pandemic unfold with a mix of concern and a quiet determination to stay informed without getting swept away by the noise. My education stopped at lower secondary, but I've always trusted the wisdom of my parents—my mother had a primary school education and my father completed upper secondary. That taught me to listen more than to argue, especially when it comes to healthcare. I've found that the most important thing is to focus on what's practical: staying healthy, helping neighbors, and being a good example for my children and grandchildren. I don't get into heated debates online because I spend only a few times a week on social media, and I've learned that the best way to manage discussions is to keep them grounded in facts and kindness. I pray that God guides us all to make choices that protect our health and the health of others, and I've seen how much difference it makes when people work together, even in small ways. response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend roughly a few times weekly on social media

response_parsed.opinion: As a 90-year-old retired man from Kansas City who's been curious about the world but not deeply involved in modern technology, I've watched the pandemic unfold with a mix of concern and a quiet determination to stay informed without getting swept away by the noise. My education stopped at lower secondary, but I've always trusted the wisdom of my parents—my mother had a primary school education and my father completed upper secondary. That taught me to listen more than to argue, especially when it comes to healthcare. I've found that the most important thing is to focus on what's practical: staying healthy, helping neighbors, and being a good example for my children and grandchildren. I don't get into heated debates online because I spend only a few times a week on social media, and I've learned that the best way to manage discussions is to keep them grounded in facts and kindness. I pray that God guides us all to make choices that protect our health and the health of others, and I've seen how much difference it makes when people work together, even in small ways. When I hear people talking about vaccines, I think of the stories my father told me about how he helped his community during the flu pandemics in the 1940s—always with a focus on doing what's right, not what's easiest.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: The persona is a 90-year-old retired man from Kansas City with a lower secondary education, raised by parents with different educational backgrounds. He's curious but not overly involved in social media, with high neuroticism indicating sensitivity to stress. His religious beliefs as a Christian and hobbies like volunteering and knitting suggest a practical, community-oriented approach. The opinion focuses on practical, community-based actions rather than online debates, drawing from his lifetime of experience and faith. The reasoning emphasizes his age, education, values, and the importance of quiet, practical engagement over emotional reactions.
response_parsed.tone: Calm, reflective, community-focused, and grounded in personal experience with a touch of faith and practical wisdom
citations: Personal anecdotes from the persona's lifetime experiences
Christian values of compassion and service
